data.content_ref: inline:{"analogy":{"adopted_commitment_refs":[],"disanalogies":["The bridge operates on evidence assembly, while general model calls operate on reasoning content","The bridge has a two-stage workflow, while simple calls may not need two stages"],"overturn_conditions":["If the bridge pattern is specific to evidence grounding and not generalizable to all call sites","If formal state is legitimately changed during some model calls by design"],"shared_structure":["Freeze-assemble-call-assert-unchanged-commit pattern","Typed interfaces prevent content from becoming control","Append-only events as the sole mutation path for formal state"],"source_memory_refs":["731938a568fa4a6200024c0bcf743cf46f14b0ead5088c5e33ef1ac2c558d5b1","7e8d35aabffc101bab4fd5da9c2db5ff619e57b70233f140c703335e45d58213","bf597433295f50d98c674f9244734bc0c9b3a89d3f1fd49f4a5d697f8a02977e"],"transfer_claims":["The bridge's formal-state-unchanged assertion should be generalized to all model-call sites as a boundary enforcement mechanism","The typed separation of evidence assembly from reasoning should apply to all harness-model boundaries"]},"claim":"The strongest deterministic control architecture treats the entire system as a state machine where the harness owns all transition semantics and the model is a pure content function. The present failure is not a single bug but an architectural boundary leak: observe-only prose criticism (crit.py:31-69) is recorded as scrutiny without warrant or formal status effect, which is correct, but the absence of a typed warrant interface means future prose could inadvertently alter status if the boundary is not enforced by the type system. The architecture should enforce C5 (prose cannot alter routes/budgets/phases/status) through typed interfaces: criticism output is CriticismProse{observations, disanalogies, overturn_conditions} with no StatusEffect type; only the harness can produce StatusEffect through adjudication. The grounded bridge (bridge/harness.py:286-457) is the template: it freezes formal state, assembles evidence, runs a typed two-stage workflow, and asserts formal state is unchanged. All model-call sites should follow this pattern: freeze, assemble, call, assert-unchanged, commit.","counterconditions":[{"case":"If the type system cannot enforce the absence of StatusEffect in model output, the boundary is conventional not structural","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If some model calls are legitimately allowed to propose state changes (e.g., repair), the pure-content-function model is too restrictive","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If the bridge pattern is too expensive to apply at every call site, performance may force shortcuts","eval":"observation"}],"definitions":[],"derivation":[],"formalization_refs":[],"mechanism":"Deterministic components: harness state machine owns all transitions (C2); manifest freeze (C1); append-only events (C2); replay materializes canonical state (C3); stopping eligibility in deterministic software state (stop.py:1-180); continuation verifies manifest identity (continuation.py:1-190). Model-call components: each model call is one bounded responsibility (C4) producing typed content; adapter enforces strict wire contract (adapter.py:300-430); budget enforced before dispatch (budget.py:79-158). Typed interfaces: ModelCall{input_context, wire_contract} -> ModelResult{typed_content, no_status_effect}; HarnessTransition{event, state_change, adjudication} -> StatusEffect. Transition semantics: model calls are side-effect-free content functions; harness transitions are append-only events; formal state changes only through adjudication. Manifest/events/replay implications: all consequential transitions logged (C2); replay produces identical formal state (C3); scratch non-authoritative (C8). Evidence and security behaviour: evidence acquisition distinct from reasoning (C7); bridge assembles evidence separately from reasoning (bridge/harness.py:286-457); adapter rejects mechanism drift. Migration: audit all model-call sites for boundary leaks; introduce typed wrappers that assert formal state unchanged after each call; no change to model output content. Retained open-ended freedom: model produces any content within its typed responsibility; harness adjudicates. Strongest likely failure mode: a future model-call site bypasses the typed wrapper and directly mutates formal state. Falsifying tests: assert no model-call site produces StatusEffect; assert formal state digest is unchanged across every model call; assert replay produces identical formal state with model outputs stubbed; assert criticism prose has no formal status effect in the event log.","premises":[],"schema":"deepreason-reasoning-envelope-v1","scope":{"covers":[],"excludes":[]},"simulation_refs":[],"uncertainties":[]}
